name: openEuler SIGs | openEuler SIG Center | openEuler
description: This page provides the complete list of openEuler community SIGs with members, repositories, SIG applications, and meetings. Anyone can participate. The openEuler community is organized by SIGs for better management and workflow.

name: openEuler SIG List
description: The complete list of openEuler community SIGs with members, repositories, SIG applications, and meetings. Anyone can participate.

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler SIGs (Special Interest Groups) are the basic collaboration units of the openEuler open-source community. Each SIG focuses on code maintenance, review, and release work for a specific technical domain.

mainEntity.name: How to join an openEuler SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: To join an existing SIG, simply submit a PR to the SIG repository to start contributing. To create a new SIG, submit an application PR to the openEuler/community repository, which will be formally established after Technical Committee review.

mainEntity.name: How many SIGs does openEuler have?

acceptedAnswer.text: The openEuler community has multiple SIGs covering core technical areas such as kernel, virtualization, cloud native, embedded, AI, and security. The exact number can be found on the SIG list page.

mainEntity.name: What are the roles in openEuler SIGs?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main roles in SIGs include: Maintainer (responsible for final code merging), Committer (has merge permissions for specific areas), and Contributor (participates in contributions via PRs).
